question: [File: 'File Name: web3/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/web3/throttle/ThrottleManagerImpl.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: throttle()] Can an unprivileged external user submit a ContractCallRequest with gas set to exactly 10,000 (equal to GAS_SCALE_FACTOR) so that scaleGas() returns 0, causing gasLimitBucket.tryConsume(0) to always succeed and completely bypass gas-based throttling, allowing unlimited high-cost requests that exhaust node CPU/memory and shut down 30%+ of processing nodes?
